title: Would you name your children after a EVA character
display_title: Would you name your children after a EVA character
wikitext:
[]: Would you name your child(ren) after a EVA character?             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:09 pm I would if i'm that into EVA. Having a daughter named Rei or Asuka or Misato would be cool.^-^ A son named Shinji...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:15 pm is sure way to make him target of every bully he even encounters

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:18 pm Heavens no, i mean aside from Evangelion names often being Japanese it might make you less "Eva Geek" and more "Mentally Insane" but for a moment i want to be insane, I've always liked the name Asuka, it's a cute sounding name. oh and Lilith [sp?]

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:20 pm Two words: I would but my country doesn't like foreign names...

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:26 pm If I were to have a daughter, I probably would.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:45 pm Why father? Why did you give me that name?! ... Why did I... Oh yeah... For the lolz!

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:48 pm Maybe if I had a daughter, her middle name would be Rei (much like my brother's middle name is take from The Thing of "The Fantastic Four"), but that's about it her first name would be something normal

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:50 pm Rei isn't a normal name?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:51 pm my middle name is Michael, so whatever

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:56 pm Would you name your child (ren) after a EVA character?

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Wed Dec 30, 2009 8:58 pm I would if I married a Japanese girl straight from Japan (you know, the kind with the accent), that way it would be more....normalish. Other than that, no.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:16 pm Rei and Lilith are considered normal names, though the former is spelled differently. Just fyi.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:19 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rei and Lilith are considered normal names

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:19 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rei and Lilith are considered normal names, though the former is spelled differently. Just fyi.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:22 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rei and Lilith are considered normal names, though the former is spelled differently. Just fyi.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:51 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rei and Lilith are considered normal names

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:52 pm Xard wrote: Lilith? WTF? Who names her children after mother of demons?

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Dec 30, 2009 9:57 pm The only name that sounds normal enough to name a person that isn't from Japan or a different country is Adam. I'm sure if someone in the US named a child Gendo, the child would hate their parents with a passion when they get older.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Dec 30, 2009 10:22 pm I'm naming my first child Bardiel, regardless of sex.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Dec 30, 2009 10:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Would you name your child (ren) after a EVA character?
